pages.raw_text:
All railroads throughout the
Northwest have big gangs at work
battling with washouts and snow
slides as the consequence of yester-
days Chinook and today's rain.
Numerous washouts are reported
all along the line of the o. r. & N.
road Telegraph and telephone
wires are down along different Sec
tons of the line. All trains on the
main line have been annulled. The
Wallace branch is the only one fit
for traffic in this part of the coun-
try and stub made up to connect
with the Wallace train at Tekoa Is
running as far west as Winona.


The Northern Pacific is reported
in fair order this morning, having
been cleared some time after mid
night, and trains may be able to go
through to Portland today If that
city remains out of water.


Trains will be started east this
evening. one due to leave here at
4 o'clock and one at o'clock.


No serious trouble Is reported on
the Great Nrthern today. but trains
are considerably behind time from
the east on account Of Ice covered
rails on eastern divisions. Snow
slides block the way through the
Cascades and no trains proceed fur
Their west than Leavenworth.
